slides.HOOK_SUB: How do AI models like ChatGPT actually work? →
slides.BODY_1_TEXT: At its core, a Large Language Model (LLM) is just playing a massive game of autocomplete. It looks at the text you gave it and predicts what the next most likely word is.
slides.BODY_2_TEXT: LLMs learn the patterns of human text by reading billions of documents, books, and articles. This helps them understand context, grammar, and even coding languages.
slides.BODY_3_TEXT: Despite seeming intelligent, LLMs don't have thoughts or consciousness. They use complex math and probabilities to string together words that look right based on their training.
slides.CTA_TEXT: I share real-world AI and Data Science lessons every week. Hit follow so you don't miss the next one.
